name: Fuentes de Andalucia from Marchena - circular via the Via Verde de la Campina
name_original: Fuentes de Andalucía desde Marchena – circular por la Vía Verde de la Campiña
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Marchena traverse the Sevillian countryside, characterized by undulating cereal fields and extensive olive groves. The region features the River Corbones and the Marchena Greenway, a former railway line converted into a cycling path. This landscape offers a mix of flat and rolling terrain, showcasing agricultural areas and natural settings like the Ribera del Corbones. Best touring cycling routes around Marchena The most popular touring cycling route is Fuentes de Andalucia from Marchena - circular via the Via Verde de la Campina, a 26.4 miles (42.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows sections of the Marchena Greenway through agricultural landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fuentes de Andalucía – Écija loop from Fuentes de Andalucía, a difficult 39.7 miles (64.0 km) path. This route offers a longer exploration of the Sevillian countryside, connecting towns and traversing varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Arrival in Marchena – Fuentes de Andalucía loop from Marchena, a 29.0 miles (46.7 km) trail leading through olive groves and farmland, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Marchena is defined by undulating cereal fields, extensive olive groves, and the Marchena Greenway. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Marchena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.95 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Marchena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Marchena?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Marchena, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.95 stars from nearly 50 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Marchena?
A: The region around Marchena, known as La Campiña, features picturesque, undulating cereal fields and extensive olive groves. You'll find a mix of flat and rolling terrain, with routes traversing various surfaces including paved roads, gravel, and ground. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchena Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a former railway line, offers a peaceful path through agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate or difficult, there are options for easier rides. For example, sections of the Marchena Greenway offer a gentler experience. Overall, 17 routes are classified as moderate, and 1 is easy, providing choices for varying fitness levels.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more demanding ride, there are 18 difficult touring cycling routes around Marchena. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes de Andalucía – Écija loop from Fuentes de Andalucía&lt;/a&gt;, a 64 km path that offers a longer exploration of the Sevillian countryside with varied terrain.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Marchena?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988099423/fuentes-de-andalucia-from-marchena-circular-via-the-via-verde-de-la-campina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes de Andalucia from Marchena - circular via the Via Verde de la Campina&lt;/a&gt;, which is 42.5 km long and follows sections of the Marchena Greenway.

Q: What historical and cultural attractions can I explore along touring cycling routes in Marchena?
A: Marchena is rich in history. Along your rides, you can explore cultural attractions such as the Church of San Juan Bautista, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sevilla Gate&lt;/a&gt;, the Castillo de la Mota – Ducal Palace, and the ancient Marchena Walls. The nearby settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes de Andalucía&lt;/a&gt; is also notable for its 18th-century Andalusian manor architecture.

Q: Are there natural features or wildlife to observe while cycling in the Marchena region?
A: Yes, the region offers natural beauty. The River Corbones flows nearby, and the Ribera del Corbones provides natural settings with trails. For nature enthusiasts, the Lantejuela Endorheic Complex Nature Reserve is a significant feature, offering opportunities for birdwatching, including sightings of flamingos and other migratory birds.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists near the touring routes in Marchena?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists are not detailed, Marchena, as a historic town, typically offers public parking options. It is advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient and secure parking spots, especially near popular starting points for routes like the Marchena Greenway.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Marchena?
A: The Sevillian countryside, including Marchena, experiences hot summers. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, with milder weather ideal for longer rides through the olive groves and cereal fields. Winter can also be suitable, though days are shorter.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Marchena?
A: Routes that utilize the Marchena Greenway, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrival in Marchena – Fuentes de Andalucía loop from Marchena&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through olive groves and farmland, are often suitable for families due to their generally flatter and more accessible terrain. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Marchena?
A: While many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's essential to check local regulations for specific routes, especially those passing through nature reserves like the Lantejuela Endorheic Complex. On general paths and greenways, dogs are often permitted if kept on a leash. Always ensure your dog is fit for the distance and weather conditions.

Q: Are there cafes, pubs, or accommodation options for cyclists in Marchena?
A: Marchena is a historic town with amenities for visitors. You'll find various cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the town center, perfect for a break or a meal. There are also accommodation options ranging from hotels to guesthouses, catering to different budgets for an overnight stay after your cycling adventures.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Marchena?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.95 stars. Reviewers often praise the scenic beauty of the undulating cereal fields and extensive olive groves, the peaceful experience of cycling along the Marchena Greenway, and the opportunity to explore charming towns and historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4835418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrival in Marchena&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
The beautiful arrival at Marchena indicates the end of the route of the Vía Verde de la Campiña.
Attention: this area is usually much more frequented than the rest of the route due to its proximity to the urban center.

This is a very beautiful stretch of the greenway that represents the vast landscape of the Sevillian countryside: a region full of history and nature. Archaeological remains, beautiful towns and lagoon areas are interspersed with extensive crop fields.
If you are in Fuentes de Andalucía, take a walk through its alleys to see manor houses - beautiful examples of Andalusian manor architecture from the 18th century.

Marchena was declared a Historic-Artistic Site in 1966, among the monuments that stand out are: the church of San Juan Bautista (one of the most interesting in the province of Seville, Gothic-Mudejar style with Renaissance elements) and the Puerta de Sevilla also called the Arco de la Rosa (one of the access doors that the walled perimeter of Marchena had).
